observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to
prepare a Schedule of Unreleased/Unclaimed Checks at the end of
every year and submit the same to Municipal Accounting Office for
appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting
Municipal Treasurer continue to exert more efforts on the cause of
the discrepancy and effect the necessary adjustments to bring into
agreement the balances presented in both records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to exert
more efforts in tracing back the prior-years’ subsidiary accounts to
establish the details of the recorded balances therein and draw a
journal entry to record the three (3) parcels of land totaling
₱69,174.00.

We recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to request
from the Management an appropriation to process the transfer of
the ownership of land from the seller to the Municipal Government
to prevent any disputes and fraud.

We recommended that the a.) Municipal Assessor in coordination
with the acting Municipal Treasurer exert more efforts in securing
second copies of other supporting documents such as but not
limited to Deed of Absolute Sale or Donation whichever is
applicable and submit thereafter to the Accounting Office for
recognition in the Agency’s books; and b.) Municipal Accountant
draw an adjusting journal entry to record the seven (7) road lots
and the condition be properly disclosed in the financial statements
to enhance the user’s understanding of the affected accounts, and
ensure complete supporting documents for future purchases of
road lots as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the a.) Local Chief Executive issue a
designation order to reconstitute the composition of the Inventory
Committee and the latter to exert more efforts to substantiate the
PPEs with unknown or unidentified balances. Moreover, conduct
complete physical count to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances in accordance with the rules and procedures
stated in COA Circular No. 2020-006. Prepare the RPCPPE and
submit to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
b.) Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the Road Network
Ledger Card for all Road Networks and RPLCs for the other real
properties on record and the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare
and maintain the equivalent Property Cards; and c.) Municipal
Accountant adequately disclosed in the Notes to Financial
Statements the Road Networks and its components in accordance
with Annex B of COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
General Services Officer require the suppliers and contractors to
provide warranty security after the acceptance of the delivered
supplies for goods and date of issuance of the Certificate of Final
Acceptance by the Procuring Entity for the infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require
the submission of the certification from the Municipal Mayor or his
authorized representative for the traveling expenses claimed by
the JO personnel of the LGU and ensure the complete supporting
documentation on the succeeding claims before it is processed for
payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all check
disbursements are properly charged to their appropriate funds and
aligned with the intended purpose for which the funds were created
to prevent depletion of other fund accounts. Further, closely monitor
the usage and availability of checkbooks for each fund to ensure
timely requisition of these from the depository bank. Lastly, the
Municipal Treasury Office and Budget Office conduct regular
reconciliations to promptly identify and address any inconsistencies
or discrepancies in the use of fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
ensure the transfer of the unexpended balance of the MDRRMF to
Special Trust Fund at year-end to reflect the correct individual
balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the MDRRM Officer agreed to include in the
LDRRMFIP all the PPAs to be funded from the prior years
unexpended balance of the Special Trust Fund. Fast track the
implementation of the financial assistance received from
Development Bank of the Philippines and consider the unexpended
balance of the two (2) other PPAs to be realigned to new disaster
related activities in the succeeding year.

We recommended that the a.) GFPS conduct gender analysis
through administration of Harmonized Gender and Development
Guidelines (HGDG) for the cost of PPAs that need to be attributed
in the GAD budget as required in Item No. 3.0(C.4)(1)(a) of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC; b.) Municipal Accountant, Acting
Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer, and Local Chief Executive
refrain from processing and facilitating payment for transactions
without clear gender issues sought to be addressed. They may refer
to the LGU's GPB and the provisions of the JMC for assessment; and
c.) GAD Focal Person submit legal basis or justification for the
payment of salaries of the barangay workers amounting to
₱200,600.00 for further evaluation.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System agreed to
regularly update the LGU’s GAD database and sex- disaggregated
data as basis in identifying gender issues in the GPB and
formulating gender-responsive PPAs to address those issues.

adjusting entries to recognize the prior year’s cash collections totaling
₱172,458.78 as a prior period adjustment in the Equity account in order
not to overstate the affected income accounts in CY 2025.

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer and the Accounting Office to
review and reclassify the road infrastructure assets into their
respective components (e.g., road lot, road pavement,
drainage, slope protection);

each road component individually, based on its useful life
and using the straight-line method;

regularly update the Local Road Inventory Report and Road

construction date, cost, physical condition, and
components; and

disclosures related to the road network system in the Notes
to the Financial Statements.

Designate completely prepare and maintain the Property Cards
and the Municipal Accountant update and maintain the PELC of all
PPE accounts. The latter may consider hiring of additional
manpower subject to the approval of Local Chief Executive.

properly account in the Agency’s Book.

5.8 We recommended that the Disposal Committee conduct

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the Municipality’s Management, request
authority from the Provincial Government of Dinagat Islands or,
if necessary, execute a Memorandum of Agreement regarding
the utilization of the ₱150,000.00 and ₱100,000.00 project funds.
Likewise, submit to the Provincial Government a complete set of
liquidation reports and record the necessary adjusting journal
entries in the accounting books upon completion of the intended
projects funded by these amounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to continue to
locate the subsidiary records, schedules and other pertinent
documents supporting the receivable balances. Seek assistance
from the previous accountants and other municipal personnel who
may have knowledge on the whereabouts of these documents,
especially from the MSWD Officer as regards the Other
Receivables account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Substantiate the Loans Payable-Domestic account by tracing
and establishing the lacking subsidiary ledgers, records and
other pertinent document supporting the account.
Reconstruct the records thereof with reference to the
available documents such as the cashbook, disbursement
vouchers and supporting documents, and other
records/documents that may be sourced on file and from
other LGU offices; and
b. Obtain additional information from the Office of the
President, reconcile with the LGU’s records in the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office and settle the balance of the loan from the
Office of the President.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Revert to the General Fund, with the assistance of the
Municipal Treasurer, the unexpended LGU
Counterparts/Equity and of which the corresponding projects
had already been completed with subject amount of
P336,674.12 to be used for priority local government
programs and projects of the Municipality;
b. Continue to trace back and identify the source agency of the
project Construction of Sr. Citizen Building and return the
unexpended balance of P22,819.00. When the amount is
proven free from any obligation, revert the amount to the
general fund to be used for priority local government
programs and projects of the LGU;
c. Verify if the programs/projects DA – SELAP (No. 11) to the
1st Tranche for Primary Care Revitalized (No. 19), were
complete or on-going. Conduct extensive effort to identify the
source agency of the programs/projects that were deficient of
information. With the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer,
return to the source agency the balance of the completed
programs/projects; and
d. Conduct regular monitoring of the status on the individual
subsidiary ledger accounts composing the Due to NGAs
account to avoid misrepresentation of the accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal
Entry Voucher to reclassify the Due to LGUs totaling P500,346.92
to Trust Liabilities account of P441,933.05 and appropriate Income
account in the general fund of P58,413.87.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to
ensure transfer of the unexpended balance of the MDRRMF to
Special Trust Fund at year-end to reflect the correct individual
balance of the Cash and Liability Accounts in the Financial
Statements and to make sure of cash back-up for DRRM programs
and projects.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
continue reconciling the balance of the cash in bank account
in their books by tracing back the prior years’ transactions
specially the CY 2016 balances which were absent of records;
and
b. The Municipal Treasurer to update her cashbook regularly
and make note of the book reconciling items so that
appropriate adjustments will be effected therein and for easy
reconciliation with the accounting records.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a. Fortify collection effort once information regarding the
receivable balances are available. Request assistance from
the MSWD Officer to collect the balances under the Other
Receivables account; and
b. Request for a write-off before COA if collection of the
receivables is too remote in spite extensive and fortified effort
to locate pertinent records and documents as well as
collection thereof, subject to the guidelines and procedures set
forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016.

We recommended that Management, through the Sangguniang
Bayan authority and assistance of the Municipal HRM Officer,
prioritize the creation of the three (3) mandatory plantilla positions
as staff and the filling up and approval of the same in order to
achieve the minimum statutory personnel requirements of the
LDRRM Office as required in the applicable provisions of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 for efficient and
effective delivery of services to the constituents.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC Secretariat,
to submit to COA the documents as proof that public bidding was
initially adopted by the LGU to procure the above-mentioned
goods like the invitation to bid, PhilGEPS postings of bids and
awards, BAC resolutions on various stages of public biddings,
Notices of Award (NOAs) and Notices to Proceed (NTPs), if there is
any. The Municipal Accountant is enjoined to check the
completeness of supporting documents of the financial transactions
before payments be made.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Chairperson advise the BAC Secretariat to post the
RFQs in the PhilGEPS website and at any conspicuous place
for a period of at least three (3) calendar days and prepare
BAC Resolution recommending award of each contract upon
determination of the Lowest Calculated and Responsive
Quotation for procurements under the shopping mode of
procurement with subject amount of more than P50,000.00;
b. BAC to attach the proof of PhilGEPS posting as well as the
BAC Resolution to the DVs; and
c. Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of
supporting documents before approving payments thereof.

We recommend that Management, with the assistance of the
Municipal Accountant and MSWD Officer, to fast track the
formulation of its own clear and comprehensible guidelines
covering the payments of AICS to prospective clients to set as
parameters in the grant thereof.

We recommended the GFPS to:
a. Include only in the GPB and its utilization the programs and
activities that will address clear and evident gender issues and
concerns as required in Item 4.1.C.1.4 of PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and
b. Use the HGDG assessment tool in attributing a portion or the
whole budget of the agency’s major programs/projects to
GAD fund. Other social services PPAs of the Agency which
do not directly address GAD issues and concerns could be
defrayed from the general fund.

1.9 We recommended that the Head of PDRRMO, OPAG, PTO and PVET to:

the inventories mentioned in Annex E, to GSO for consolidation
and eventually to the Accounting Office as basis of derecognition
thereof from the books; and
b. Prepare and issue Certification covering the issued/distributed
inventories that were deficient of supporting documents,
signifying that the items were indeed given to the intended
beneficiaries. The Certification will be submitted to GSO and to
the Accounting Office as basis for dropping the inventories from
the books.

2.8 We recommended that the Provincial Accountant reconcile the
balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds
accounts by effecting the necessary adjusting entries with due
consideration of the reconciling items noted above and as shown in
Annex F in order to bring the two reciprocal accounts into
agreement.

3.10 We recommended that the Provincial Accountant draw a Journal
Entry Voucher to adjust/reclassify the following in the respective
book of accounts:
a. 20 percent Development Fund - Other Receivable Accounts of
P1,392,000.00 to Loans Receivable – Others;
b. Trust Fund - Due to Other Funds totaling P1,163,668.40 to Trust
Liabilities account; and
c. General Fund Proper – Allowance for Impairment – Other
Receivables of P371,232.84 to Other Deferred Credits account.

4.7 We recommended that the Provincial Accountant seek personal
assistance from the service provider to immediately modify and add
features to the existing accounting system to address the deficiency
noted and for the former to reclassify the loans receivable totaling
P1,160,000.00 from Current Asset to Non-Current Asset in
consonance with Section 76 of IPSAS No. 1

5.8 We recommended that the PCF Custodian ensures full liquidation
of the PCF at year-end in compliance to Section 48 of NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I.

6.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Consider imposition of collateral on loans granted to
cooperatives to ensure recovery of loss in case of default of
payments therefrom;
b. Institute measures to improve the collection rate of the loans
receivable like, but not limited to evaluation and close
monitoring of the financial status of the borrowers, provision of
statement of account to borrowers prior to respective due date,
and ocular visits to cooperatives from time to time;
c. Initiate the immediate collections of long overdue accounts
through strict imposition of the provisions of the Loan
Agreement like filing of legal remedies against the defaulting
borrowers; and
d. Conduct investigation on cooperatives reported under state of
bankruptcy or non – operational, to determine the possibility of
collections of their outstanding loans and if found too remote,
request for dropping from the books the accounts receivables
that may be considered dormant.

7.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Advice the recipients with completed projects but without
complete records and pertinent documents to substantiate the
same to prepare and execute a Certificate signifying that the
corresponding project is existing and the fund received
intended thereto was indeed utilized to implement the same.
This would be the basis of the accounting office to drop from
the books the fund transfer made to the agency; and
b. Extend guidance in the preparation of the liquidation report
to the recipients that have no knowledge in doing the same so
that a report will be made and submitted to the Province.

8.9 We recommended that Management: (1) Reinstitute, through
PCAO, the covering of MOA or Agreement for the support/aid
funds to barangays with subject amount above P1,000.00 to ensure
implementation of the project as intended; and (2) Advise the
Accountant to record the support funds as receivable, instead of an
expense account, to maintain monitoring thereof and uphold
accountability of the funds.

9.10 We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that procurement under the Bayanihan Act should
be made from legitimate suppliers who could readily and
immediately provide/produce the goods; and
b. Collect liquidated damages from the supplier due to delays
incurred on deliveries as provided above. Make sure that
liquidated damages will be computed and collected from
delays of performance.

10.6 We recommended that Management shall submit to the Auditor the
documents as basis in deciding not to impose and collect liquidated
damages from the supplier despite the delays incurred in deliveries
for further evaluation by the Audit team.

11.6 We recommended that the BAC shall consider the taxes itemized by
the non-cooperative bidders in their bids and which the cooperatives
are exempt from to be included in the bid evaluation to have equal
footing and ensure fair and competitive bid comparison is provided
to interested and qualified suppliers in compliance to the intent of
Rule 1, Section 3 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB
Circular No. 02-2010

12.5 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson ensure the correctness
and consistency of information provided in the BAC Resolutions
and the ones posted in the PhilGEPS. Please be reminded that the
mode of procurement in the APP can be amended every six (6)
months or as often as needed as provided in R.A. No. 9184 through
a BAC Resolution duly approved by the Head of the Procuring
Entity.

13.9 We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that all donations in-kind received from outside sources
be coursed through to the PGSO. If not possible, and for
practical reasons the delivery of the goods/items need to be to
the distributing units or office like the PDRRMO, PHO or
PSWDO, the latter should furnish the former the
acknowledgement receipts of the donations in-kind and/or the
Turn-Over and Acceptance Form as proof of receipts of such
donations. The PGSO should likewise be furnished of the
documents prepared during the distribution of the goods to
intended beneficiaries like the proof of receipt by and
distribution to the beneficiaries and inventory of the remaining
undistributed items. This will cause the PGSO to consolidate
the information and prepare the Summary/List of Donations
Received, Distributed and Balances.
b. Direct the PGSO to prepare and submit the Report to the
NDRRMC, through the Office of the OCD, copy furnished the
Auditor in a manner prescribed in Item II of COA Circular
2020-009. A copy of the Report must likewise be furnished to
PACCO for recording in the books the remaining or
undistributed donations; and
c. Advise the PACCO to take up in the books the remaining or
undistributed donations based on the Summary/List of
Donations Received, Distributed and Balances prepared by the
PGSO.

14.13 We recommended that Management direct:
a. The Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer
to prepare and submit the Monthly Reports on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF to COA on or before 15th day after the
end of each month; and
b. The Provincial Accountant and Treasurer to ensure transfer of
the unexpended balance of the PDRRMF to Special Trust Fund
at year-end to reflect the correct balances of the Cash, Liability
and Equity Accounts in the Financial Statements and to make
sure of cash back-up for DRRM programs and projects.

15.5 We recommended that the Management: (1) Fully adopt the
features of the human resource information system; (2) Advise the
PHRMO personnel to reconcile the discrepancies observed between
the information system and the manual system regarding the
number of available leave credits; and (3) Approve only requests for
monetization with retention of at least five (5) leave credits.

observations.recommendation:
The Management to direct the concerned offices to strictly
observe the timely submission of accounts and the required
schedules for calendar year 2020 transactions in accordance with
Section 39, 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No.2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.

The Management to: a) prepare a catch-up plan and fast track the
implementation of the above-mentioned priority development
projects; b) refrain from charging the 20% DF of expenditures that
do not contribute to the attainment of socio–economic development
and environmental management and those that do not partake the
nature of investment or capital expenditures; and c) direct the
Provincial Planning and Development Coordinator to enhance
proper planning in the allocation and utilization of 20% DF to ensure
proper implementation of the projects and to meet the need of the
intended beneficiaries in timely manner.

1.8	We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of PPE, Biological Assets and Inventory at the end of every year in order that appropriate adjustments of the accounts affected be promptly effected, leading to a reliable and fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statement.

2.7	We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting journal entries to take up the error and reconcile the balance of Advances to Officers and Employees with its subsidiary ledger to come up with the correct balances in the Financial Statement.

3.7	We recommended that management call the attention of the concerned Accountable Officers for immediate liquidation for their long overdue cash advances or refund in favour of the government the unliquidated amount.

4.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer’s enhance planning and measures to enforce tax collection in order to meet or even increase to a desirable level the tax collection efficiency.

5.5	We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Networks System.

b. The Municipal General Service Officer – Designate to: Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map; Regularly maintains and updates the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

c. The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.; and

d. The Municipal Accountant to: Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card; Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; Disclose in the Notes to FS the length covered by the system, additions and reductions, major repairs and regular maintenance for the year.

6.4	We recommended to require the GFPS to adopt the required format in the preparation of the GAD Accomplishment Report for uniformity and so as to help the management with essential information in assessing the implementation of the GAD PPAs whether or not it addressed the gender issues and women’s empowerment.

8.6	We recommended to improve the utilization rate of the 5 percent MDRRMF with the prescribed guidelines, and inform the MDRRMC of the unimplemented PPAs so that they could be of help in the implementation or in the reprogramming of PPAs deemed no longer priority of the Municipality.

9.9	We recommended that the financial assistance to barangays for the construction of the infrastructure projects be covered with Memorandum of Agreement to provide basis for monitoring the proper use of such government funds and be liquidated by the implementing agencies through the submission of required documents mentioned in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Also, future financial assistance to barangays should be booked-up as Due from LGUs.

10.7	We recommended that Management ensure strict compliance on the laws, rules and regulations on the monetization of leave credits, specifically Section 22 of Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292 and submit the lacking required supporting documents above to ensure propriety of the transactions in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe proper procedures in the cancellation of stale check and to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the cancellation of the said stale checks.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of PPE and its Biological Assets at the end of every year in order that appropriate adjustments of the said accounts affected be promptly effected, leading to a reliable and fair presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue Cash Advances or refund in favor of the government the unliquidated amount, if applicable.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to provide basis in setting up receivables at the beginning of the year, thereby complying the requirement of the provisions of Section 269 of RA 7160 and Section 20, NGAS, Volumes I for LGUs.

We recommended that Management exert extra efforts to enforce collection of the dormant accounts.  In the event that collection is no longer feasible after exerting all legal remedies,   a request for write-off of accounts be submitted to COA, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect immediately the required adjustments in the books of accounts in order to revert the Accounts Payable which are already considered as dormant accounts.
We recommended that Management observe strict compliance with the provision of Section 119 of PD 1445.
We recommended that Management fast track and prioritize the implementation of the above-mentioned projects in order that the intended beneficiaries can avail of the benefits of the projects.

We recommended that Management identify qualified stakeholders to be appointed as members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in order for the same to properly discharge the mandated function of the organization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management to strictly observe the following course of actions:
Conduct of complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be done by the Inventory Committee.
Maintain the required PPE ledger cards and making sure that all properties are completely reported in the inventory report.
Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine correctness and/or any losses occasioned by theft or other casualty. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the inventory report and accounting records, the necessary adjustment/corrections should be made.
Submit complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for the LGUs, Volume 1.
Enjoin the Management to strictly adhere to Section 13, 20, 27, and 25 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

Direct the BAC to require the contractors to post retention money equivalent to 10% of every progress payment or special bank guarantee equivalent to 10% of the contract price as required in Section 62 of Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions. Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling or liquidation thereof has been made of the previous cash advance.

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Engineering Office to review the reasonableness of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) on the on-going procurements and adhere to the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 22 Series of 2015 for the upcoming projects.
Direct the Procurement Officer-Designate to indicate in all purchase orders complete information required in COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set by DSWD in the implementation of the AICS program. All financial assistance should be supported with the documents specified in the DSWD guidelines to support all the disbursement made.
